introduction:
The Ration Card in Rajasthan is issued by the Food and Civil Supplies Department, Government of Rajasthan. It is a vital document under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) for providing subsidised food grains through Fair Price Shops (FPS) / Ration dealer network across the state.

In Rajasthan, the Public Distribution System (PDS) is heavily integrated with the Jan Aadhaar Card (formerly Bhamashah). Linking your Ration Card with the Jan Aadhaar is almost mandatory to seamlessly avail benefits, pensions, health insurance (Chiranjeevi Yojana), and free food kits.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Service charge at e-Mitra is fixed (typically ₹40 to ₹50) for online application
  • Processing usually completes within 15-30 working days
  • Possessing a Jan Aadhaar Card speeds up the entire process tremendously
  • e-Mitra Kiosks are the official and most efficient route for applying in Rajasthan
  • Aadhaar linking is absolutely mandatory for all family members
  • Food Dept Helpline: 1800-180-6127
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Ration Card

Note: Original documents must be scanned at the e-Mitra kiosk. Ensure your Jan Aadhaar Details are updated first.
Identity Proof
  • Jan Aadhaar Card / Bhamashah Receipt
  • Aadhaar Card (of all family members)
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
Address Proof
  • Water / Electricity Bill (recent)
  • Telephone Bill
  • Gas Connection Passbook
  • Rent Agreement / Property Tax receipt
Income & Other Documents
  • Income Certificate (Required strictly for BPL / AAY mapping)
  • Passport size photographs of Head of Family (preferably Female)
  • Old Ration card/Surrender Certificate (if moving from another address)
Card Categories (Colour Guide)
  • AAY (Yellow Card): Antyodaya (Poorest of the poor)
  • BPL (Dark Pink): Below Poverty Line families
  • State BPL (Green Card): Subsidized by State Government
  • APL (Blue/Green Cards): Above Poverty Line (Mainly for ID)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply via Authorized e-Mitra Kiosk

In Rajasthan, the government has routed all major citizen services, including Food & PDS, through the e-Mitra network. It acts as the assisted online portal for everyone.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate an e-Mitra Center
    Find a nearby registered e-Mitra kiosk. Take all original documents, including the Jan Aadhaar or its enrolment slip.
  2. Provide Details to Operator
    The operator logs in using SSO IDs. Provide your details and tell them you want to apply for a "New Ration Card" (Form A).
  3. Scanning & Uploading
    The operator will scan all proofs, the Head of Family's photo, and map your Jan Aadhaar correctly with the Ration Card portal.
  4. Pay the E-Mitra Fee
    A fixed nominal government-approved fee (typically ₹40-50) is charged. Collect the computerized E-Mitra Receipt.
  5. Barcode Reference
    The receipt bears a Token/Application Number (usually 16 digits). Save this to track status.
  6. Department Verification
    The application goes to the respective Block Development Officer (BDO) or Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) who handles verification.
Tip: Ensure your Jan Aadhaar is error-free before applying, as data is automatically fetched from it. Avoids 90% of rejections.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline via SSO / SDM Office / Panchayat

Though E-Mitra is highly advised, individuals with a valid SSO (Single Sign-On) ID can submit applications themselves, or fall back to traditional Panchayat-level filing.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. If doing Self-Service (Online-Offline via SSO):
    Log into sso.rajasthan.gov.in -> e-Mitra portal -> Services -> Avail Services -> New Ration Card. Fill carefully, upload docs, pay fee.
  2. Using Traditional Offline Form:
    Obtain the traditional New Ration card application form from the Gram Panchayat or the Tehsil / SDM Office.
  3. Fill and Enclose Copies
    Fill the details manually matching the Jan Aadhaar. Attach self-attested copies of address proof, income proof, and photographs.
  4. Submit and Verification
    Submit to the Gram Sevak/Panchayat secretary (rural) or District Supply Officer counter (urban). The Patwari or Gram Sevak will conduct physical verification of your residence and income limits.
  5. Smart Card Retrieval
    Upon successful verification, the digitized smart ration card will be transferred to your E-Mitra/Panchayat for collection.
Processing Time: Purely offline mode can take longer due to manual transit. Average 30-45 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application Status

Visit food.rajasthan.gov.in and choose Ration Card \u2192 Track Application Status. Enter your receipt/token number.

Collect from e-Mitra

Once officially verified and printed, the physical copy is delivered back to the e-Mitra kiosk. Go and collect your Ration Card book / smart card upon an SMS alert.

Check Eligibility (NFSA)

If you fall under the NFSA category, ensure your name appears in the NFSA digitized lists on the web portal to receive subsidized ration without interruption.

Rajasthan Sampark Grievance

Facing unreasonable delays, demands for bribes from the dealer? Register a complaint immediately on the Rajasthan Sampark portal or call 181.
